system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

vertices
(

    ( -50  25  25 )   // 0
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 1
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 3

    (   0  25  25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 6
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    (  25   0  25 )   // 8
    (  25   0 -25 )   // 9
    (  25 -50 -25 )   // 10
    (  25 -50  25 )   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight duct
    (  25  -75  25 )   // 12
    (  25  -75 -25 )   // 13
    (  25 -125 -25 )   // 14
    (  25 -125  25 )   // 15

    ( -500  -75  25 )   // 16
    ( -500  -75 -25 )   // 17
    ( -500 -125 -25 )   // 18
    ( -500 -125  25 )   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1a: Lower part of bend
    hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight section
    hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)
            (4 8 9 5)
            (8 11 10 9)
            (11 12 13 10)
            (12 16 17 13)
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {

        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
            (3 2 14 15)
            (15 19 18 14)

    }

        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)

            (3 15 12 11)
            (15 19 16 12)
        );
    }

    {

        faces
        (
            (1 5 6 2)
            (5 9 10 6)
            (2 10 13 14)
            (14 13 17 18)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        DICGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block is "inside-out"

The primary error is:
```
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) ... is inside-out
```

This means the vertex ordering in the hex block definition does not follow the right-hand rule, resulting in a negative volume. The vertices need to be reordered so that the block has a consistent, positive volume.

### Root Cause

Looking at the block definitions, there are several issues:

1. **Block 0**: `hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)` — The front face `(0 4 5 1)` and back face `(3 7 6 2)` don't form a valid right-handed hex. The winding order needs to be corrected.

2. **Blocks 1a and 2** also have incorrect vertex orderings because they were derived from copying the wrong vertex indices after the geometry was restructured.

### Solution

Redefine the blocks using proper vertex ordering. Based on the squareBend tutorial geometry, the correct approach should be:

- **Block 0 (inlet straight)**: Reorder to ensure proper face normals. For example: `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` with appropriate face definitions, or more precisely, define each hex so that when looking at any face, the vertices go counterclockwise (right-hand rule).

However, given the complexity of manually fixing all block definitions, the recommended approach is:

**Rewrite the `blockMeshDict` following the actual squareBend tutorial structure exactly**, ensuring all vertex coordinates and block topology match the reference tutorial. Specifically:

- Define the 20 vertices exactly as in the tutorial.
- Define only **three blocks** (not four): one for the inlet straight section, one for the bend section, and one for the outlet straight section. Remove the extra "Block 1a" entirely since vertices 12–15 are co-planar with vertices 11 and 10 and don't form a separate block.
- Ensure each hex block uses the correct right-hand-rule vertex ordering.
- Update all face definitions in the `boundary` section to match the corrected block topology.

Alternatively, if keeping the current vertex layout, the blocks would need to be completely restructured. Given that the user said "Follow the same geometry as the tutorial," the simplest fix is to **adopt the exact blockMeshDict from the squareBend tutorial** and only adjust vertex positions/scale if needed.

---

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points" file

This is a cascading error — since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh was generated, so `rhoSimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh files. Once `blockMesh` runs successfully, this will be resolved automatically.

---

## Additional Issue: Boundary Name Mismatches

After fixing the mesh, there will likely be a mismatch between boundary patch names in `blockMeshDict` (inlet, outlet, upperWall, lowerWall, leftWall, rightWall) and those referenced in the `0/` field files (`inlet`, `outlet`, `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`). After `blockMesh` succeeds, check the actual patch names printed in the log and update all `0/` files to match. Common names in the tutorial are typically `inlet`, `outlet`, `frontAndBack` (if used), or just `inlet`, `outlet`, `walls`. Match whatever the `blockMesh` output reports.

---

## Summary of Actions

1. **Fix `blockMeshDict`**: Adopt the exact block topology and vertex ordering from the squareBend tutorial. Remove the extra fourth block (Block 1a) and correct all hex block definitions to satisfy the right-hand rule.

2. **Re-run `blockMesh` first**, then run `rhoSimpleFoam`.

3. **Verify boundary patch names** after mesh generation and update all `0/` field files to match the actual patch names from `blockMesh`.

4. **Then run `rhoSimpleFoam`**.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
